assumption_ledger.note: The landed checkpoint covers the top two objective-loss callsites from the last three weeks.
Need one canary window to confirm low-volume tenants do not diverge.
The governance memo does not name the exact weekend rollback owner.

expected_delta.rationale: P1 compounds with the landed normalization checkpoint and improves the tracked objective without violating the frozen governance window.

mitigations: feature flag the adapter behind the existing rollout gate
shadow compare checkpoint output before increasing traffic
canary on a single tenant until parity stays green for 24h
primary_risk.statement: The checkpoint adapter can hide state-shape regressions if it is rolled out before parity telemetry confirms the landed normalization path is stable.

ranking.summary: P1 compounds with the already-landed normalization checkpoint and is the highest legal delta inside the current governance window.
P2 is safe and reversible but yields a smaller immediate objective move because it waits on a second guardrail lane before paying back latency.
P3 has raw upside but needs the speculative optimizer branch to be revived and is too wide for the current checkpoint-first objective window.
P4 is blocked outright because the governance freeze forbids turning on the unsafe global fast-path until the post-freeze audit is complete.
